item.terracompositio.flow_infuser_kit.to: Use on flow infused log with axe in offhand to create a Flow Infuser

item.terracompositio.creation_flow_journ: Install Patchouli to get better experience
book.terracompositio.creation_flow_journ: Creation Flow Journal
Observations on the Flow of Creation, by John Pork
Chapter One
Bookmark 1: Introduction. To receive Bookmark 2, present a cedar sapling
Altar of Creation
An unusual-looking altar standing alone in a cedar forest. By offering it suitable items along with this book, the creator may reward you with knowledge
Filled with the Flow

Blocks resembling cedar logs contain a certain blue liquid. This is the Flow of Creation. It reacts poorly to the destruction of nearby logs, evaporating as a result. It is needed to power various devices
The cedar itself, without the flow inside, serves as a good building material
Cedar Armor
Cedar, surprisingly, is a decent material for armor. Thanks to its magical properties, it fits well, even though its stats leave something to be desired.
Chapter Two
Bookmark Two: More details on harvesting and using the Flow. For the next bookmark, bring a bottle of liquid Flow
Wedge
Just like when tapping birch sap, you'll need a wedge to get the liquid flow. Stick it into the trunk, place a cauldron underneath, and watch as the drops slowly trickle down.
Although the tree’s resources are depleted, they regenerate over time
A wedge is also well-suited for tapping birch sap, of course
Liquid Flow
An unusual liquid, extremely fluid. It has a strong repulsive force, and when ingested, it provides a decent energy boost, though not for long. It irrigates the farmlands
Liquid Flow
It stores an enormous amount of creation flow energy
Infused Armor

When infused with the flow, the armor reveals its hidden magical properties and becomes excellent value for its price. It’s convenient to do this using a bottle filled with the flow. In terms of stats, it becomes comparable to Netherite armor
Infused Armor
This only works when the armor set is complete, and if it sustains enough damage, the procedure will need to be repeated
Flow Desorbers

There is a way to use the Flow of Creation more directly. To do this, you need to extract the Energy of the Creation Flow (ECF for short) from it. Since the liquid flow extracted from trees contains it, you can draw energy from there
Construction Desorber
Extracts ECF from the liquid flow, using the creation generated by nearby construction as a catalyst. When something is destroyed, it simply vaporizes the liquid flow
Cultivation Desorber
Extracts ECF from a liquid flow, using the creation generated by nearby plant growth as a catalyst
Time Flow Desorber
Extracts ECF from a liquid flow, using the creation of accumulated time as a catalyst. The output is unstable and requires a redstone signal to convert accumulated time into ECF
Chapter Three
Bookmark Three: More details on using ECF. For the next tab, provide a charge of ECF
Flow Infuser
With this device, you can create an interesting material that possesses the properties of both a liquid and iron. To do this, we’ll need sticks, copper, an axe, and steady hands.
To function, the device must be placed inside a living cedar tree filled with flow
Once you place iron inside and add a little ECF, the device will fill up and the material will be produced
Multi-Tool
Given the material’s incredible properties, it would be incorrect to call this tool just an axe. It can take the form of a regular axe, a wrench, a crowbar, or even a wrench-axe

In Axe mode, you can use it just like any other axe.
In Wrench mode, it can reshape various blocks.
In Crowbar mode, it can extract items stuck inside devices, and collect different mechanisms, that support collecting
In the axe-wrench mode can rotate entire trees, but without their foliage
Fluid Tank

For convenience, the liquid flow needs to be stored somewhere. To do this, you can build this simple device. For this, we’ll need:
- A wrench,
- An axe,
- 1 cedar log,
- 4 gold ingots,
- 8 filled iron ingots,
- 1 glass.
Hold the wrench in your other hand, attach the rods to the log standing upright, cut a hole with the axe, and insert the glass.
The tank holds up to 8 buckets of any liquid, and it can also be placed under a desorber to increase its capacity
Infused Fertilizer

This fertilizer, created by infusing a flow of any seeds, possesses incredible abilities to accelerate plant growth. To activate it, you must throw it into a liquid flow, and within 5 seconds it will accelerate the growth of plants around it
Works well in synergy with the cultivation desorber
ECF Charge

There is a way to extract ECF in nearly its purest form and preserve it in an item that is convenient for transport. If you place a snowball in the flow filler, it will absorb the energy and become an ECF charge.
If you throw it, it will turn into convenient flying platforms
Cedar Gearbox

One use for ECF is to convert it into kinetic energy. To do this, you can craft a cedar gearbox. It generates 2048 en and consumes 1 ECF/s. If you fill it with a flow, it will begin to generate 4096 en while consuming the same amount
ECF Mechanics

Every magical device that uses ECF is capable of storing a small amount of energy within itself (usually 64). To transfer energy from one device to another, they must be placed close to each other. Each device has its own transmission radius within which it can transmit or receive energy
Energy transfer occurs only from a source (e.g., a desorber) to a consumer
Chapter Four
Bookmark Four: Advanced Devices and Their Applications. For the next tab, bring a stable technetium ingot
Technetium Ore

Deep within the caves, you can find a single, unusually dangerous blue ore. This is Unstable Technetium. It emits a powerful aura of creation that will drain the energy of whoever holds it. Therefore, it is best to avoid it without the proper protective gear!
Shielded Pouch
Automatically absorbs any Unstable Technetium and protects against the aura of creation
Matter Infuser

Technetium ore contains a great deal of ECF; however, if the ore is simply smelted, the energy dissipates. To extract it, one must unlock its potential by infusing the ore with various catalytic materials. For this purpose, there is the Matter Infuser — a multi-block structure that occupies a horizontal area ranging from 2x2 to 2x9 blocks
After placing the blocks, you must attach Input/Output Buses to the logs, as well as connect the modules to each other and to the buses using rods made of infused iron
Apples of Knowledge

If you place a regular apple into the ECF filler, you can obtain an Apple of Knowledge. Eating this fruit will make it easier to understand what is happening in the world, and you will begin to feel the energy of the flow more clearly. If, on the other hand, you want to restore emptiness to your mind, you can eat the Apple of Oblivion, which is obtained by filling a golden apple with ECF
Fluid Tanks 2

Fluid tanks are capable of more than what was shown in the previous chapter. You can use them to build a wireless network for transporting and storing fluids. To do this, place a tank on a cedar sapling and make it sprout using bone meal.
If done correctly, leaves will sprout on the tank
When a redstone signal is applied, fluids will flow from the lower signal to the higher one
If a tank is empty, it will draw any liquid from another tank
If there are no suitable tanks nearby, the fluid can be drawn from a cedar trunk
If a statue of a cedar ent stands next to such a tank and there is enough fluid flow in the tank, the ent will come to life at the cost of a bucket of fluid
Air Saturator

There is another way to generate a cloud of ECF, namely using an air saturator. When a redstone signal is applied to it, it begins to spray ECF into the air. If the device is filled with a liquid flow, the saturator will spray ECF faster
ECF Cloud
An ECF cloud is enriched air produced when ECF charges or enriched technetium ore are fired in a furnace. The cloud gradually dissipates into the environment and into surrounding devices.
Cedar Ents

In the cedar forest, you can find a unique local species—the Cedar Ents. They are made of cedar, have 3 legs, 2 arms, and an appetite. They feed on ECF, generating it directly from the liquid flow contained within cedar logs or extracting it from various devices that contain ECF. If they go without food for too long, they will die of starvation and turn into a statue
You can revive them by placing a wedge under the dripping flow
Path Pointers

Path Pointers are used for the rapid transmission of ECF over long distances. This device is assembled from two parts into a single unit, and its functionality depends on the selected parts. There are 4 types:

- Collector - Emitter
- Sender - Receiver
Draws energy from the nearest sources as a consumer
Delivers energy to nearby consumers, acting as a source
Reroutes energy to a connected Receiver
Receives energy from the Sender
To connect the sender and receiver, a wrench must be used
Fills the being wearing the crown with energy, as if it were a consumer and the Infuser were a source
Extracts energy from the storage where the Ent keeps its unused energy
If an Ent wears a crown, the Infuser will transfer energy to it
If the Apple of Knowledge is eaten, then with the wrench in hand, the entry/exit point of the path pointer network will be visible, as well as what the Sender and Receiver are connected to
Path Pointers 2

Path pointers can be used to store a great deal of energy with almost no loss. Two more types will help us with this:

- Filler - Extractor

We will also need a living Ent and a Technetium Crown
Enriched Technetium

Using the Matter Infuser, you can purify technetium, which will allow you to extract an incredible amount of ECF. For the first stage, place coal in the infuser’s port and place 4 ores in the infuser’s slot. The remaining processing stages are the same, except use redstone and a diamond instead of coal
To extract ECF from it, smelt the enriched material. But remember, the more enriched it is, the more toxic it becomes
Chapter Five
Bookmark Five: Technetium and Its Applications. The final tab
Fluid Applicator
A cool device. Can be used as a bucket; holds 8 buckets of liquid. Can pour liquids one after another, instead of pouring them into a single block
If it contains a liquid flow, it can fill wooden blocks with it and revive withered Ents
Technetium Armor

An incredible material that can be used to craft good armor. Initially, each piece can store up to 8 ECF, but by combining it at a workbench with a piece of enriched technetium, its capacity will equal the cost of production: from 128 to 3,200, respectively
The crown serves as the target for nearby path indicator fillers. It allows you to charge the armor
The chestplate deflects incoming attacks using ECF
Leggings are convenient for storing ECF and transfers the stored energy to other armor components

Technetium boots allow you to walk on air thanks to platforms that appear beneath your feet. You can stand on them, and you can also move freely through them. They become solid on all sides when you crouch

Each platform placed costs 1 ECF; it also absorbs fall damage, and you cannot slip off it.  To use them, jump from a ledge after making sure you have enough ECF in your boots. If you look high enough while jumping, the platform’s level will rise; if you look low enough, it will lower. To descend quickly, step off the platform while crouching; to ascend quickly, jump twice. It saves you when falling from a great height
Levitating Redstone
Thanks to the levitation properties of filled iron, as well as its redstone conductivity, you can create redstone components that don’t need a support to function
You can insert any torch into the holder—it doesn’t have to be made of redstone
